title: chthamalia decumbens
intro: Chthamalia decumbens, also known as the creeping cucumber tree or creeping sea rosemary, is a flowering plant species native to Australia. The medicinal properties of Chthamalia decumbens include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating skin infections and wounds. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity. Chthamalia decumbens can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves for topical application, allowing for a range of medicinal uses.
